page: Amazon Web Services c6gd.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores on the 64-bit ARM64 architecture, utilizing the AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and is bundled with 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate average multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 48571.1 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 11706.0. The instance demonstrates solid performance in database operations, achieving 20441.91 in PassMark database tests and 40.61 million operations per second in Redis SET operations. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 958.0 and a weak single-threaded gzip compression rate of 20.31 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth is strong for small cached blocks at 1894.03 GB/sec but drops to 173.34 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, resulting in a weak PassMark memory score of 1830.4.

The c6gd.8xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency through its high physical core density and dedicated ARM64 architecture, which avoids hyperthreading overhead. However, workloads requiring x86-specific instruction sets will face compatibility limitations, as the processor lacks AVX, AVX-512, and AMX extensions. Given its balanced multi-core performance and high-capacity local NVMe storage, this instance is suited for multi-threaded applications, database hosting, static web serving, and software compilation tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services c6gd.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated physical cores on the ARM64 architecture, powered by a 2.5 GHz AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show average multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 48571.1 and strong database capabilities, including 40.61 million Redis SET operations per second. However, single-core performance is weak, as indicated by a Geekbench single-core score of 958.0, and the platform lacks AVX or AMX instruction sets. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, and local database operations that benefit from dedicated ARM cores and high-speed local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6gd.8xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 32 dedicated Graviton cores, 64 GB RAM, and 1900 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering steady multi-core and database performance.

tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated physical cores and high-capacity local NVMe storage for multi-threaded workloads.

1900 GB of integrated local NVMe SSD storage
64.0 GB of system memory with 2.0 GB per core ratio
Suited for multi-threaded databases, web serving, and software compilation
Lacks AVX, AVX-512, and AMX instruction sets
